_configVersion_comment: DO NOT MODIFY: This field is used by NeoEssentials for automatic config updates. Changing this may cause config corruption.
_comment: Discord Authentication Configuration - Maps Discord roles to Dashboard permissions AND Minecraft permissions
enabled_comment: Enable/disable Discord authentication (requires Simple Discord Link mod)

oauth2._comment: Discord OAuth2 settings for 'Login with Discord' button in the web dashboard. Create an application at https://discord.com/developers/applications
oauth2._step1: Go to https://discord.com/developers/applications and create or open your application
oauth2._step2: Under OAuth2 > General, copy your Client ID and Client Secret
oauth2.clientId_comment: Discord Application Client ID (visible on the OAuth2 General page)
oauth2.clientSecret_comment: Discord Application Client Secret — keep this private, never share it
oauth2.redirectUri_comment: Must exactly match one of the redirect URIs registered in your Discord application. Use your public IP/domain and dashboard port.
oauth2.scopes_comment: OAuth2 scopes to request. 'identify' = basic user info. 'guilds.members.read' = server roles (required for role mapping). Do not change unless you know what you are doing.

requireLinkedAccount_comment: Require users to link their Minecraft account via Discord bot (bot sends DM with link instructions)
allowAutoRegistration_comment: Automatically create dashboard accounts for Discord users on first login
defaultRole_comment: Default role for users with no Discord role mapping (ADMIN, MODERATOR, or VIEWER)

roleMapping._comment: Map Discord role IDs to Dashboard roles. Use role IDs (right-click role in Discord > Copy ID) NOT names. Requires Discord Developer Mode enabled.
roleMapping._example: Discord Role ID -> ADMIN, MODERATOR, or VIEWER
roleMapping._how_to_get_id: Enable Developer Mode in Discord Settings > Advanced > Developer Mode. Then right-click any role and select 'Copy ID'

roleHierarchy._comment: Define which dashboard roles each Discord role ID grants (optional, for multi-role users). Use role IDs, not names.

whitelistedRoles_comment: Only allow Discord users with these role IDs (empty = allow all). Use role IDs, not names. Example: ['1234567890123456789', '9876543210987654321']
blacklistedUsers_comment: Block specific Discord user IDs from logging in. Example: ['123456789012345678']

permissionSync._comment: Automatically sync Minecraft permissions based on Discord roles (Build #124+, Fixed in #132)
permissionSync._how_it_works: When players join, their Discord roles are retrieved and mapped to Minecraft permissions. Works with LuckPerms or built-in permission system.
permissionSync.enabled_comment: Enable/disable Discord role -> Minecraft permission synchronization
permissionSync.syncOnJoin_comment: Automatically sync permissions when player joins server (recommended: true)
permissionSync.syncMethod_comment: Permission grant method: 'auto' (detect LuckPerms/built-in), 'luckperms', 'builtin'. Auto-detect recommended.

permissionMappings._comment: Map Discord Role IDs to Minecraft permission nodes. Players get ALL permissions from ALL their Discord roles (permissions are combined).
permissionMappings._important: Use Discord Role IDs (numbers), NOT role names. Enable Developer Mode in Discord to copy role IDs.
permissionMappings._how_to_get_role_id: Discord Settings > Advanced > Developer Mode ON, then Server Settings > Roles > Right-click role > Copy ID
permissionMappings._role_mod_plus: Mod+ (709353103530655857) - Moderator with temp password generation

permissionMappings_common_examples._admin_role: ['neoessentials.*'] - Full access to all NeoEssentials features
